steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage contrasts two groups: non-tool-using otters eat energy-poor snails, while tool-using otters access high-quality mussels and clams. The question asks what follows if both groups have the same energy needs.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'non-tool-using otters suffer more tooth damage': The passage shows tool-users avoid tooth damage by using rocks instead of biting. ✗
  • Choice B — 'tool-using otters gain no fitness benefit': The passage explicitly states tool-using otters gain fitness benefits from higher-quality prey. ✗
  • Choice C — 'non-tool-using otters must eat more prey': If snails are energy-poor and both groups need the same energy, non-tool-users must consume greater quantities to compensate. ✓
  • Choice D — 'non-tool-using otters can access hard-shelled prey by biting': Non-tool-users cannot crack hard shells without tools — this scenario is impossible per the passage. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
